title: Smoky Paprika Canadian Sweet Potato Stir-fry
description: A sweet and crispy crowd-pleaser featuring smoky paprika, steamed sweet potatoes in a nut-free-friendly stir-fry that's easy to prepare.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Rinse sweet potatoes and pat dry.
Slice sweet potatoes into coins about ⅛ inch thick, leaving skin on for extra texture. Season with salt and pepper.
Arrange sliced sweet potatoes in a single layer on an aluminum foil-lined baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil and toss gently to coat evenly.
Bake in preheated oven for 15 minutes or until lightly browned and tender.
Meanwhile, mix cornstarch with water to make a slurry (a thick sauce).
Heat a wok over medium-high heat. Add shallots and garlic; stir-fry for about 2 minutes until fragrant but not browned.
Add bell peppers and remaining sweet potatoes back into the wok, stirring well to combine with vegetables.
Pour in cornstarch slurry and mix quickly to cook out excess moisture from the veggies. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes or until sauce thickens slightly.
Toss with chopped green onions just before serving.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
